title: nepeta argolica subsp. vourinensis
intro: Nepeta argolica subsp. vourinensis, also known as Greek catmint, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing anxiety and insomnia, alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, and supporting digestive health. Nepeta argolica subsp. vourinensis can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties.
